Tunnel Configuration

Manage your Cloudflare Tunnel connection

Enter the token provided by the Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard.

Open Cloudflare Zero Trust
Advanced Configuration

Custom identifier tag shown in Cloudflare dashboard

Software name displayed in Cloudflare dashboard (default: cfui)

Connection protocol to Cloudflare network

Shutdown grace period (e.g., 30s)

Preferred connection region

Maximum connection retry attempts

Port for metrics server

Local IP address to bind for outgoing connections to Cloudflare edge (optional)

Disable TLS certificate verification for backend services (not recommended for production)

System Logs

System ready. Waiting for configuration...

Remote Tunnel Manager

Manage Cloudflare-hosted ingress rules independently from the local runner.

Disabled
Remote manager configuration

Can also be provided by CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID or CLOUDFLARE_APP_ID.

Changing this field loads the selected tunnel configuration.

API Token is recommended. Blank secret fields keep the saved value.

Required Cloudflare API permissions Account · Cloudflare Tunnel · Edit Zone · Zone · Read Zone · DNS · Edit Use account-scoped API Token when possible.

No token saved.

Create a scoped API token at Cloudflare Dashboard → My Profile → API Tokens.

Your Cloudflare account email address.

No key saved.

Find your Global API Key at Cloudflare Dashboard → My Profile → API Keys.

Ingress Rules

No remote config loaded.

Add published application

Create a public hostname route like the Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel form.

Public hostname

Select a Cloudflare zone or type a domain manually.

Optional path matcher. Leave empty to publish the whole hostname.

Service

Use host:port for HTTP/HTTPS/TCP/SSH/RDP, a socket path for UNIX, or a status code.

Additional application settings

Dynamic DNS

Automatically update DNS records when your public IP changes.

Disabled

Configure API credentials in Remote Tunnel Manager first.

Current Public IP —
IPv4 —
IPv6 —
DDNS Settings

One URL per line.

DDNS Records

Sync Log

MCP Access

Let AI clients operate cfui through the Model Context Protocol.

Disabled

Access tokens

Tokens are shown once when created. The list only shows masked values.

MCP configuration

Connect an MCP client to the endpoint below and use a bearer token generated here.

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "cfui": {
      "url": "http://localhost:14333/mcp",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_TOKEN"
      }
    }
  }
}

Available tools include status, config update, tunnel start/stop, recent logs, and remote tunnel ingress rule management.

Paste your own token or leave blank to generate a strong token.

Copy this token now

It will not be shown again after you leave this page.

Features

Enable or disable optional feature tabs. Disabled features are hidden from the navigation.

Remote Tunnel Manager

Manage Cloudflare-hosted ingress rules via API.

DDNS

Automatically update DNS records when your public IP changes.

MCP Access

Let AI clients operate cfui through the Model Context Protocol.
